summary.en: The creative, fiercely independent heart of Las Vegas, with a gritty-chic aesthetic of converted warehouses, vibrant murals, and a community of artists.
Historic Vegas with a modern, indie facelift, offering a dynamic blend of restored mid-century neon, intimate cocktail bars, and a block-party atmosphere.
A sprawling, energetic culinary paradise packed with authentic and innovative food in unassuming strip malls; this is where locals go for the real deal.

name.en: The Old Vegas Steak & Martini Trail

summary.en: A time-traveling experience into the Las Vegas of the Rat Pack and the Mob, connecting iconic old-school haunts with red leather booths and ice-cold martinis.
A clandestine walking tour through the city's most creative district, focused on the thrill of discovering hidden and low-key bars.
A journey of escapism and contrast, connecting kitschy, rum-fueled fantasy tiki bars with the gritty, no-nonsense soul of an old Vegas dive.

description.en: An essential stop with a whimsical vibe and a seasonal masterpiece of a cocktail menu featuring creative, unusual concoctions.
Widely considered the city's best brewpub, offering a fantastic range of craft beers and surprisingly good food in a spacious, industrial setting.
A fun, high-energy spot perfect for a messy and delicious meal, specializing in massive, inventive burgers and boozy milkshakes.
An unassuming ice cream shop that hides a moody, intimate bar behind a freezer door, perfect for a secret nightcap.
The corridors between Main and Commerce Street are an ever-changing outdoor gallery of massive murals from local and international artists.
A fantastically quirky tiki bar designed to look and feel like a sunken pirate ship, offering an immersive and playful escape.
A dedicated listening lounge perfect for a relaxed evening, appreciating high-fidelity sound in a chill, focused environment.
A legendary, no-frills dive bar on the edge of the district, famous for cheap drinks, loud music, and spirited karaoke nights.
Las Vegas's oldest freestanding bar, a historical landmark where patrons once watched atomic bomb tests from the roof.
A rum-soaked, Polynesian-themed oasis offering a welcome, kitschy escape from the Fremont Street chaos.
A true speakeasy hidden inside Commonwealth, offering an intimate, transportive cocktail experience that feels like a genuine discovery.
A solid gastropub with a famously eclectic, art-filled back patio, perfect for weekend brunch or a casual dinner.
A modern throwback to the glamorous steakhouses of vintage Vegas, located inside Circa Resort and known for its high-end quality.
Housed in the iconic glass dome of The Plaza, this steakhouse is named for a famous former mayor and offers landmark views.
The intersection of Fremont and 6th Street, the perfect vantage point to see the contrast between the cool vibe of Fremont East and the light-show canopy.
The entire street is a de facto museum of vintage signage, with restored signs on the facades of old motels and bars.
A famous historic hotel featuring a beautifully restored vintage neon sign, emblematic of the neighborhood's character.

name.en: Màs Por Favor Taqueria Y Tequila

description.en: A vibrant taco shop with a major secret: a hidden, cavelike speakeasy in the back specializing in tequila and mezcal.
One of the most celebrated and creative restaurants in Las Vegas, serving an evolving journey of American cuisine with global influences.
A representative shopping plaza where you can explore the sheer density and variety of restaurants, from Korean BBQ to Vietnamese pho.
Cultural hubs filled with unique snacks, exotic produce, and imported goods you won’t find anywhere else.
The building itself is a historical landmark—a former federal courthouse that hosted famous mob hearings.
The path between the area's locations is a de facto outdoor museum of some of the city's most famous and historic neon signs.
A low-lit, intimate bar that feels like a secret even in plain sight, where bartenders craft cocktails tailored to your tastes.
The 15-minute walk between tiki bars is key to the experience, as you see the city's character shift from quiet murals to buzzing downtown.

prompt.en: Find a piece of street art and take a photo that makes it look like you're part of the scene.
Capture a detail that perfectly represents the neighborhood's 'gritty-chic' aesthetic.
Photograph a unique vintage item you spot in a shop window or on the street.
Take a picture of your coffee or craft beer, framed against a creative backdrop.
Find the most interesting-looking alley and capture its hidden character.
Take a photo of a building that has clearly been converted from industrial use into something new and creative.
Find a sign or storefront that screams 'fiercely independent' and capture its essence.
Take a photo focusing on a single, vibrant color you find in an unexpected place.
Use the afternoon sun to create an interesting shadow selfie against a textured wall.
Find a worn-in bar stool or quirky chair that looks like it has stories to tell.
Photograph a unique drink or dish that looks like a work of art.
Try to capture a scene that feels authentically local, away from any tourist crowds.
Take a close-up shot of three different textures you find on your walk: brick, metal, and paint.
As evening falls, capture the glow of a single interesting light source.
Find a piece of graffiti or text art and photograph it from an interesting angle.
Take a portrait of a friend or yourself illuminated only by the light of a neon sign.
Capture a single image that shows a historic building next to a modern one.
Take an artistic, top-down photo of your drink on a unique bar top.
Photograph a scene that captures the energetic, block-party atmosphere of the street.
Find a piece of architecture or a sign that looks like it's straight out of the 1950s.
Take a photo looking from the quieter part of the street towards the massive light canopy, capturing the contrast.
After a rain, or using a puddle, capture the reflection of the vibrant street lights.
Find a classic car or a vintage-style sign and take a photo that feels timeless.
From inside a bar, take a photo looking out onto the bustling street.
Photograph a vintage motel or bar sign, focusing on its unique typography or design.
Take a selfie that embodies the 'effortlessly cool' vibe of the neighborhood.
Get a mouth-watering shot of your meal, making it look as delicious as it tastes.
Find a doorway or entrance that looks exclusive or hidden and snap a picture.
Capture the way the setting sun interacts with the neon signs and historic buildings.
Capture the diverse energy of the area by taking a candid (but respectful) photo of the street life.
Photograph the most unusual or interesting ingredient you can find in an Asian market.
Take a picture of a menu that features a language you don't speak.
Capture a wide shot of a strip mall that shows the incredible density and variety of businesses.
Take a photo of a dish so beautifully presented it looks like art.
Find a dessert shop and photograph its most colorful and tempting treat.
Capture an image that shows this is an authentic, local's Chinatown, not a tourist trap.
Find a restaurant with a neon sign and take a picture of it at dusk.
Photograph an unassuming storefront that looks like it might hide a culinary gem inside.
Take a photo down a long aisle in a supermarket, showcasing the variety of products.
Arrange your meal and drink into a perfect top-down 'flatlay' photo.
Find a spot bustling with locals and capture the lively atmosphere.
In a food-centric plaza, find and photograph a non-food business.
Take a close-up shot of a delicious-looking street taco.
Take a picture of your full plate of food, and then another of the empty plate after you've finished.
Find three different kinds of noodles, rice, or dumplings and photograph them together.
Take a selfie in a mirror, adjusting your collar or tie like you're heading to a show in 1960.
Get a classic, sophisticated photo of an ice-cold martini.
Capture the ambiance of a classic steakhouse, focusing on the dark wood or red leather booths.
Find a high vantage point and take a photo of the glittering city lights below.
Take a photo of a historic building that looks like it could have been a mob hangout.
Use the neon lights to create a dramatic, film-noir-style photo with strong shadows.
Find an official historical plaque or marker and take a photo of it.
Take a photo of your shoes against an interesting floor, showing you're ready for a classy night out.
Try to capture an image that feels like it could have been taken 50 years ago.
Photograph a unique piece of architecture, focusing on its curves and lines.
Clink glasses with a friend and capture the moment with the vintage Vegas background blurred.
Find a classic or luxury car and photograph it in front of a historic building.
Capture the gritty contrast of a simple beer in a dive bar.
Get a photo of the city lights reflected in your cocktail glass.
If you're having steak, take a photo that makes it look like the star of the show.
Find a door that looks plain or hidden and photograph it, hinting at the mystery behind it.
Take a low-light photo that captures the clandestine, moody vibe of a hidden bar.
Create a silhouette selfie using the dim, atmospheric lighting.
Capture a candid shot of a bartender meticulously crafting a cocktail.
Take a photo of an object or sign that acts as a clue to a hidden entrance.
Photograph a building's exterior that looks completely different from the vibe of what's inside.
Ask a bartender to make you something unique and photograph the resulting masterpiece.
Focus on a single, interesting light fixture—a candle, a dim bulb, a neon sign—in a dark room.
Take a photo that shows the transition from a bright space to a dark, secret one.
Find a hidden patio or outdoor space and capture its secluded charm.
Take a celebratory selfie once you've successfully found a hidden spot.
Photograph a sweet treat that serves as a 'cover' for a hidden bar.
Capture a piece of street art at night, lit by a single streetlight.
Find an intimate, quiet corner and take a photo that conveys a sense of privacy and conversation.
Find a sign with a set of rules and take a picture of your favorite one.
Take two photos: one of a fantastical, colorful tiki drink, and one of a simple, no-nonsense beer.
Photograph a piece of decor that perfectly sums up the 'escapist' tiki vibe.
Capture a detail in a dive bar that shows its age and character—a peeling sticker, a worn-out bar top, or a vintage beer sign.
Take a photo halfway on your walk between the two different zones, capturing the shift in the city's character.
Get a shot of the karaoke stage, capturing the energy of a local performer.
Use the unique lighting in a tiki bar to take a portrait that looks like it's underwater.
Take a photo that makes a tiki bar look like a true oasis from the city outside.
Capture an image that conveys the loud, energetic, no-frills vibe of a classic dive.
Find the most wonderfully kitschy, over-the-top decoration and give it a glamorous photoshoot.
Take a photo of a detail that proves you're in a real local's hangout, not a tourist spot.
Capture the unique light of a dive bar—a mix of beer signs, a jukebox, and dim overheads.
Take a close-up photo of the materials of a tiki bar—bamboo, thatch, carved wood.
Find an object that looks like it belongs on a pirate ship and photograph it.
Create a photo collage of one tiki detail and one dive bar detail.
Take a selfie that captures your mood, whether it's tropical relaxation or dive bar revelry.
